raw_text:
Manager, MLOps Engineer

DataFlow Systems — Democratizing machine learning operations for enterprises worldwide

Location

San Francisco, CA (Remote-first with optional in-office)

Employment Type

Full-time

Compensation

$210,000 - $270,000 USD

The Opportunity

We're seeking an experienced Manager, MLOps Engineer to lead and mentor a team of 5-7 MLOps

engineers while driving the architecture and implementation of our next-generation model deployment

platform. This role combines hands-on technical leadership with people management responsibilities.

You'll oversee the design of production ML infrastructure, ensure best practices in model monitoring and

governance, and guide your team to solve complex challenges in orchestration, feature management,
and model lifecycle management. This is an ideal opportunity for a senior engineer looking to step into a

manager role while staying deeply technical.

About DataFlow Systems

DataFlow Systems is a rapidly growing MLOps platform company founded in 2018. We help enterprises
scale machine learning workflows across hybrid and multi-cloud environments. Our customers include

Fortune 500 companies and fast-growing startups leveraging our tools to accelerate ML model
deployment, monitoring, and governance. We're backed by top-tier VCs and have a team of 150+

engineers across offices in San Francisco and Austin.

Day-to-Day

Lead and mentor a team of 5-7 MLOps engineers, providing career development, performance
feedback, and technical guidance on complex architectural decisions

Design and oversee the implementation of scalable ML platform infrastructure using Kubernetes,

Docker, and Airflow for production workload orchestration

Establish and enforce MLOps best practices across model deployment pipelines, including versioning,
CI/CD integration, and automated testing frameworks

Architect and implement model monitoring and observability solutions using industry-standard tools
(e.g., Prometheus, custom ML-specific dashboards) to detect model drift and performance

degradation

Drive adoption of feature stores and centralized feature management platforms to standardize feature
engineering and reduce time-to-production for data scientists

Conduct quarterly planning and roadmap discussions with cross-functional stakeholders (Data
Science, Data Engineering, Product) to align infrastructure priorities with business objectives

 Lead incident response and post-mortems for production ML system failures, identifying process

improvements and mentoring team members through resolution

Champion infrastructure-as-code practices and containerization standards using Docker and

Kubernetes, ensuring reproducibility and security across environments

Evaluate and integrate emerging MLOps tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, new monitoring solutions) into the

platform stack, making technology selection decisions in collaboration with the team

You Have

6+ years of hands-on experience in MLOps, ML infrastructure, or platform engineering roles

2+ years of proven people management experience, including hiring, mentoring, and performance

management of technical teams

Deep expertise with container orchestration (Kubernetes) and containerization (Docker) in production

ML environments

Strong experience architecting and deploying workflow orchestration tools (Airflow, Kubeflow, or

equivalent) for complex ML pipelines

Demonstrated proficiency with model monitoring, drift detection, and ML observability platforms

Excellent system design and distributed systems knowledge with experience managing high-
availability production systems

Strong communication skills and ability to translate technical concepts for stakeholder audiences at
various levels

Bonus

Experience with MLflow, Kubeflow, or similar ML experiment tracking and lifecycle management
platforms

Hands-on experience building or managing feature stores and feature platforms (e.g., Feast, Tecton)

Background in DevOps or SRE with focus on ML-specific use cases and infrastructure patterns

Familiarity with model governance frameworks, data lineage tracking, and compliance in regulated
industries

Our Stack

Kubernetes   Docker   Apache Airﬂow   MLﬂow   Kubeﬂow   Prometheus & Grafana   Feature Stores (Feast/Tecton)

Jenkins/GitLab CI/CD   TensorFlow Serving / KServe   Python

What We Offer

Competitive health insurance (medical, dental, vision) with company coverage of 85% of premiums

401(k) retirement plan with 4% company match, immediate vesting

Unlimited PTO plus 10 company holidays; flexible work arrangements and remote-first culture

 Annual learning and development budget of $3,000 per employee for conferences, courses, and

certifications

Equity package (stock options) with standard 4-year vesting schedule

Parental leave (16 weeks paid for primary caregivers; 8 weeks for secondary)

Free lunch and snacks in office; stipend for home office equipment for remote employees

Mental health support through partner EAP and subsidized gym membership

Apply

Submit your resume, cover letter (optional but encouraged), and a link to your GitHub or portfolio

showcasing MLOps projects to careers@dataflowsystems.io. In your cover letter, please include 1-2

examples of ML infrastructure challenges you've led a team through and how you approach mentorship.

Applications reviewed on a rolling basis; responses within 5 business days for qualified candidates.
nice_to_have: Experience with MLflow, Kubeflow, or similar ML experiment tracking and lifecycle management
Hands-on experience building or managing feature stores and feature platforms (e.g., Feast, Tecton)
Background in DevOps or SRE with focus on ML-specific use cases and infrastructure patterns
Familiarity with model governance frameworks, data lineage tracking, and compliance in regulated
